title: Stage Extension in the Auditorium
openingLine: Hi, I need staging for performances in our auditorium and I saw on your website you make stage extensions. We don't have enough room on the existing stage for the full ensemble during concerts. Can someone come out and help me figure out what would work?
customerContext: Your auditorium stage is too small for your 80-piece concert band. Right now you set up folding chairs on the floor, which sounds terrible and looks worse. You've been frustrated for years. The principal is on board because parents have been complaining about visibility. You're hoping this is finally getting addressed.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Existing stage is 36x20 ft. We need maybe 12-14 ft of extension for full band setup.
Principal carved out $40K from the activity budget for this. That's the working number.
Want it before December concerts. About 4 months from now.
Principal already approved the budget. I just need to pick the solution.
We have storage space behind the stage — I'd want it modular and easy to deploy.
Drama club would also use it. They do thrust staging sometimes.
No one involved — just me and the principal.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Requested layout from facilities or offered field measurement
Researched purchase history before the visit
Asked about needs, budget, and timeline up front
Asked if they're working with a consultant or architect

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered help if the project is over budget
Discussed National Contracts vs. competitive bidding

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Probed for drama and other shared uses of the staging

BANT.behavior: Asked whether the project is funded and the budget amount
Confirmed install timeline tied to December concerts

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set follow-up tasks tied to the December deadline
